title: Tender Smoky Mexican Beef Birria
description: A smoky and tender Mexican dish featuring beef, parmesan cheese, and a blend of spices, perfect for a crowd, this birria is a classic Yucatecan recipe with a rajasic-friendly twist.
instructions: Heat the lard or oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the beef pieces and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the ancho chili powder, cumin, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper, and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the beef broth, tomato paste, and browned beef, and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 2 1/2 hours, or until the beef is tender.
Shred the beef with two forks and stir in the parmesan cheese.
Simmer for an additional 10 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and the birria is hot and bubbly.
Serve the birria hot, garnished with chopped fresh cilantro, if desired.
Offer warm tortillas on the side, for making tacos or dipping into the birria.
